name: Suspicious Linux Log Cleared
notes:
This rule triggers when attempts to clear logs on the system. Adversaries may clear system logs to hide evidence of an intrusion.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Clear Linux Logs
SIGMA Description: Detects attempts to clear logs on the system. Adversaries may clear system logs to hide evidence of an intrusion


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Attempt
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [shred or rm or unlink]
when the event matches Process Path (custom) contains any of [/var/log or /var/spool/mail], Command (custom) contains any of [/var/spool/mail or /var/log]
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Linux Log Cleared on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Attempt
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [shred or rm or unlink]
AND when the event matches Process Path (custom) contains any of [/var/log or /var/spool/mail], Command (custom) contains any of [/var/spool/mail or /var/log]
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Linux Log Cleared
Event description: Detects when attempts to clear logs on the system. Adversaries may clear system logs to hide evidence of an intrusion.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
